title: Sick-Day-Peppery Canadian Fish Poutine
description: A comforting and anti-inflammatory-friendly Canadian dish perfect for a sick day. Crispy fries, peppery sauce, and tender fish come together for a flavorful and satisfying meal.

ingredients.name: Fries
ingredients.notes: unsalted, unpeeled, preferably fresh or frozen

instructions: Cut the cod fillets into 1-inch pieces and season with salt and black pepper.
Dredge the fish pieces in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the fish until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Transfer the cooked fish to a plate and set aside.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter in the skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced fries to the skillet and cook until golden brown, about 5-6 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Transfer the cooked fries to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a large saucepan, combine the dashi broth and peppery sauce and bring to a simmer over low heat.
Add the French-fried onions to the saucepan and stir to combine.
To assemble the poutine, place a portion of fries in the bottom of a bowl, followed by a piece of cooked fish, and finally top with the peppery sauce and onions.
